description: Information Service was a newsletter that covered religious, social, and cultural issues in the context of Christian faith and church activities. It was established in 1920 and was published bi-weekly, (except July & Aug., and monthly in Nov.), by the Central Dept. of Research and Survey, National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America until 1969. The publication aimed to provide church leaders, scholars, and interested readers with analysis, commentary, and information on issues affecting Christian communities. The publication featured articles of various types, including interviews, sociological studies, theological reflections, and reports on societal challenges such as civil rights, hunger, and education. Subjects frequently covered included church policies, social justice movements, religious doctrine, and the role of Christianity in public life. Its content was designed to foster dialogue about the church’s position on pressing societal issues and explore the evolving relationship between religion and modern society.
subject: Philosophy & Religion
Religious Research
title: Information Service
